qoq_changes: Merchandise inventories increased to $26.20 billion from $23.45 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Operating expenses rose to $8.46 billion, compressing operating margins slightly compared to the prior year.
new_risks: Integration and execution risks related to the recent acquisitions of SRS Distribution Inc. (SRS) and GMS Inc. (GMS).
Potential supply chain and cost impacts from tariffs, trade policy changes, or international trade disputes.
Continued macroeconomic pressures on the housing market, credit markets, and consumer home improvement demand.
management_tone: Neutral; the company demonstrated resilient top-line growth but faces margin pressure from rising operating expenses and ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty in the housing sector.
analyst_note: Home Depot delivered modest revenue growth of 2.8% in Q3 2025, though higher SG&A costs led to a slight 1.3% contraction in net income. Investors should closely monitor the integration of the SRS and GMS acquisitions and the potential impact of tariffs and housing market headwinds on future profitability.
